name: demarshal_invalid_message_is_marshalable
code:
proof fn phi_1_demarshal_invalid_message_is_marshalable(data: Seq<u8>)
    requires
        !(exists |v: CSingleMessage| v.is_marshalable() && v.ghost_serialize() == data),
    ensures
        sht_demarshal_data(data).is_marshalable(),
{
}
reason: `sht_demarshal_data` uses `choose` with only `recommends` — when no valid CSingleMessage deserializes to the data, the chosen value is arbitrary and may satisfy `is_marshalable`, allowing downstream code to treat garbage data as valid

name: serialize_append_allows_arbitrary_growth
reason: The `serialize` trait ensures `data` grows by exactly `ghost_serialize().len()` bytes, but `ghost_serialize` is external_body at the trait level — if an impl's open spec disagrees with the external_body exec, the ensures are vacuously satisfied

name: csingle_message_ack_tag_collision
reason: CSingleMessage::Ack has tag 1 and InvalidMessage has tag 2 — if the macro assigned the same tag, deserialization would be ambiguous; verifying distinct first bytes confirms tag uniqueness

name: cmessage_delegate_shard_tag_distinct
code:
proof fn phi_4_cmessage_delegate_shard_tag_distinct(kr: KeyRange<CKey>, ep: EndPoint, h: CKeyHashMap)
    requires
        (CMessage::Shard { kr, recipient: ep }).is_marshalable(),
        (CMessage::Delegate { range: kr, h }).is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        (CMessage::Shard { kr, recipient: ep }).ghost_serialize() != (CMessage::Delegate { range: kr, h }).ghost_serialize(),
{
}
reason: Shard (tag=4) and Delegate (tag=5) share similar structure (KeyRange + payload) — if tags collided or serialization was ambiguous, a Shard message could be misinterpreted as Delegate

name: abstractify_net_packet_ignores_invalid
code:
proof fn phi_5_abstractify_net_packet_ignores_invalid(net: NetPacket)
    requires
        !(exists |v: CSingleMessage| v.is_marshalable() && v.ghost_serialize() == net.msg),
    ensures
        abstractify_net_packet_to_sht_packet(net).msg == sht_demarshal_data(net.msg)@,
{
}
reason: `abstractify_net_packet_to_sht_packet` always calls `sht_demarshal_data` which uses `choose` — even when no valid deserialization exists, it produces an arbitrary abstract packet that downstream protocol reasoning may treat as legitimate
